home.heroTitle: Discover Japan's most captivating sightseeing trams and distinctive trains
home.heroSubtitle: Experience the charm and elegance of Japan's most iconic tram lines. From mountain railways to historic streetcars, each journey tells a story.
home.featuredDescription: Explore our curated selection of Japan's most beautiful and historic sightseeing trams.
home.galleryDescription: Beautiful moments captured from Japan's iconic tram lines
home.byLocationDescription: Switch between regional browsing and editor-picked recommendations.
home.tabLocationHint: Browse lines by area such as Kanto, Kansai, and Kyushu.
home.tabPopularityHint: A curated recommendation list to help you discover routes quickly.
home.infoCard1Desc: Japan's sightseeing trams represent over a century of transportation heritage, from early 1900s streetcars to modern scenic railways.
home.infoCard2Desc: Experience breathtaking views of Mount Fuji, bamboo groves, temples, and traditional neighborhoods through carefully preserved tram routes.
home.infoCard3Desc: Every tram journey is a window into Japanese culture, connecting travelers with local communities and historical landmarks.

seo.introTitle: Japan Scenic Trains and Trams Guide
seo.introBody: Explore sightseeing trains, streetcars, and rail routes across Japan with region-based directories, practical route planning notes, and curated articles for first-time and returning travelers.
seo.quickLinksDesc: Pick your preferred intent first: region browsing, route type, or planning knowledge.
seo.quick1Desc: Start from Kanto, Kansai, Kyushu, and other regions to compare routes geographically.
seo.quick2Desc: Switch between streetcars, sightseeing trains, and limited express using category filters.
seo.quick3Desc: Use practical and historical context posts to plan route choices before your trip.
seo.quick4Desc: Review coverage scope and editorial direction before diving into route detail pages.
seo.regionDirDesc: Use this region-first index to jump directly to route detail pages and spread internal link equity.
seo.readDesc: Build context first with practical route planning and rail culture notes.
seo.faqDesc: Use these quick answers to avoid common planning mistakes before route selection.
seo.faqQ1: Can I rely on this site for booking decisions?
seo.faqA1: Use this site for discovery and comparison, then confirm final timetable, fare, and booking rules on official operator pages.
seo.faqQ2: What is the difference between streetcars and sightseeing trains?
seo.faqA2: Streetcars are often urban mobility routes, while sightseeing trains focus on scenic windows, themed cars, and travel experience.
seo.faqQ3: Where should I start as a first-time visitor?
seo.faqA3: Start with the regional directory, shortlist routes, read one or two insights articles, then open each route detail page.
seo.faqQ4: Does seasonality matter when choosing routes?
seo.faqA4: Yes. Mountain, coastal, sakura, autumn foliage, and snow routes vary heavily by season and daylight conditions.
seo.faqQ5: Can I browse in languages other than English?
seo.faqA5: Yes. Switch between English, Japanese, and Traditional Chinese using the language control in the header.

tramDetails.planYourVisitDesc: Ready to experience the charm of this iconic tram line? Learn more about visiting.
tramDetails.notFoundDesc: The tram you're looking for doesn't exist.

placeholder.comingSoonDesc: This page is currently being developed. We're working to bring you wonderful content about Japan's sightseeing trams.
placeholder.suggestionText: Let us know what you'd like to see on this page!

footer.brandDesc: Discover the charm and history of Japan's iconic sightseeing tram lines
footer.copyright: © 2024 Japan Trams. All rights reserved. Celebrating Japan's sightseeing heritage.

hakoneTozanRailway.name: Hakone Tozan Railway
hakoneTozanRailway.description: An iconic mountain railway featuring scenic views of Mount Fuji and Lake Ashi. The cable car and scenic train offer breathtaking views of the Hakone caldera throughout the year.
hakoneTozanRailway.history: The Hakone Tozan Railway was established in 1921 as a scenic mountain railway. It features a unique switchback railway system and cable car designed to navigate steep mountain terrain.
highlights: Views of Mount Fuji on clear days

arashiyamaTram.name: Arashiyama Tram
arashiyamaTram.description: A charming open-air tram line that winds through the scenic Arashiyama district, passing by bamboo groves, temples, and traditional tea houses.
arashiyamaTram.history: Operating since 1910, the Arashiyama tram is one of Kyoto's most romantic ways to explore the district. The open-air experience connects visitors with the natural beauty of this historic area.

nagasakiStreetcar.name: Nagasaki Streetcar
nagasakiStreetcar.description: Historic streetcars that have served the city since the early 20th century, offering an authentic way to experience local life and wartime history sites.
nagasakiStreetcar.history: The Nagasaki streetcar system, established in 1912, is the oldest continuously operating tram system in Japan. It offers authentic vintage charm with fully restored cars.

hiroshimaElectric.name: Hiroshima Electric Railway
hiroshimaElectric.description: A comprehensive tram network connecting the Peace Memorial Park, local neighborhoods, and historic sites throughout modern Hiroshima.
hiroshimaElectric.history: The Hiroshima Electric Railway has been serving the city since 1912, continuing its mission to connect communities and preserve the city's transportation heritage.

matsumotoTram.name: Matsumoto City Tram
matsumotoTram.description: A charming tram line in the castle town of Matsumoto, connecting the iconic Matsumoto Castle with the surrounding neighborhoods and traditional streets.
matsumotoTram.history: The Matsumoto tram system, established in 1961, provides convenient access to the beautiful castle town and its historic neighborhoods.

jrEastViewTrain.name: JR East View Train
jrEastViewTrain.location: Various scenic routes, primarily Kanto region
jrEastViewTrain.description: A special tourist train operated by JR East featuring panoramic windows and dining services, offering unique views of the Japanese countryside.
jrEastViewTrain.history: Launched in the early 2000s as part of JR East's Joyful Train fleet, the View Train highlights seasonal landscapes.

jrEastHolidayExpress.name: JR East Holiday Express
jrEastHolidayExpress.description: A festive excursion train providing holiday-themed decorations and special events, aimed at family trips and seasonal celebrations.
jrEastHolidayExpress.history: Part of JR East's Joyful Train series introduced to create cheerful travel experiences during holidays.

enoden.name: Enoshima Electric Railway
enoden.description: A scenic coastal tram line famous for ocean views and access to historic temples.
enoden.history: Operating since 1902, Enoden has been a beloved symbol of the Shonan coast.

eizanRail.name: Eizan Electric Railway
eizanRail.description: A mountain tram providing access to sacred sites with lush forest scenery.
eizanRail.history: Since 1925, Eizan has connected Kyoto with its spiritual countryside.

izukyuResort21.name: Izukyu Resort 21
izukyuResort21.description: A premium sightseeing train offering coastal views and luxury interiors.
izukyuResort21.history: Introduced in 1985 to boost Izu tourism.

kotoden.name: Kotoden
kotoden.description: A local tram network noted for its retro livery and cultural routes.
kotoden.history: Serving Shikoku since 1948, Kotoden is a regional icon.

toyamaChiho.name: Toyama Chiho Railway
toyamaChiho.description: A diverse railway system including mountain trams and scenic lines.
toyamaChiho.history: Founded in 1923, it serves as Toyama's transportation backbone.

hakodateTram.name: Hakodate Tram
hakodateTram.description: A charming city tram system winding through historic Hakodate.
hakodateTram.history: Operating since 1910, it's one of Hokkaido's oldest urban trams.

sapporoStreetcar.name: Sapporo Streetcar
sapporoStreetcar.description: A modern streetcar loop running through central Sapporo.
sapporoStreetcar.history: Opened in 1909 and later modernized to a low-floor network.

kagoshimaTram.name: Kagoshima City Tram
kagoshimaTram.description: A resilient tram network surviving through wars and volcanic eruptions.
kagoshimaTram.history: Operating since 1912 and famed for its durability.

okayamaTram.name: Okayama Electric Tramway
okayamaTram.description: Known locally as 'Okaden', it serves as the backbone of Okayama city transport.
okayamaTram.history: Started in 1928 and still uses some classic vehicles.

todenArakawa.name: Toden Arakawa Line
todenArakawa.description: Tokyo's only remaining streetcar line, running through nostalgic neighborhoods.
todenArakawa.history: Survived post-war cuts to remain the capital's last tram line.

kumamotoTram.name: Kumamoto City Tram
kumamotoTram.description: A historic network serving Kumamoto with five lines and nostalgic cars.
kumamotoTram.history: Opened in 1912 and known for its resilience through earthquakes.

toyohashiTram.name: Toyohashi Tram
toyohashiTram.description: Operated by Toyohashi Railroad, famous for its compact city loop.
toyohashiTram.history: Started in 1925 as part of local urban expansion.

izukyuSaphirOdoriko.name: Izukyu SAPHIR Odoriko
izukyuSaphirOdoriko.description: A luxury sightseeing train offering spectacular coastal and mountain views.
izukyuSaphirOdoriko.history: Redesigned in 2015, it combines vintage charm with modern comfort.

kuramaRailway.name: Kurama Railway
kuramaRailway.description: A scenic vintage railway through Kyoto's mountain temples and forests.
kuramaRailway.history: Operating since 1927, it's a heritage railway beloved by tourists.

tateyamaKurobe.name: Tateyama Kurobe Alpine Route
tateyamaKurobe.description: A cable car, ropeway, and trolleybus system through the Japanese Alps.
tateyamaKurobe.history: Completed in 1971, it's one of Japan's most scenic mountain journeys.

asoRailway.name: Aso Railway
asoRailway.description: A scenic mountain railway surrounding Mount Aso's caldera.
asoRailway.operatingHours: Limited daily service with scenic observation cars
asoRailway.history: Founded in 1988, it offers nature tourism through volcanic landscape.

sakurajimaRailway.name: Sakurajima Scenic Railway
sakurajimaRailway.description: A railway offering pristine views of active volcano Sakurajima Island.
sakurajimaRailway.history: Established in 1974 to promote volcanic area tourism.

kochiTram.name: Kochi Tram (Tosaden)
kochiTram.description: One of Japan's oldest electric tram networks, featuring charming vintage cars.
kochiTram.history: Operating since 1904, it has witnessed over a century of Kochi's development.

tokushimaTram.name: Tokushima City Tram
tokushimaTram.description: A modern tram system serving Shikoku's eastern gateway city.
tokushimaTram.history: Modernized in 1999 as part of urban transportation improvements.

jrHitachiTokiwa.name: HITACHI & TOKIWA
jrHitachiTokiwa.description: High-speed limited express trains connecting Tokyo with destinations in Ibaraki and Tohoku.
jrHitachiTokiwa.history: Modern comfortable trains representing JR East's state-of-the-art service.

jrAzusaKaijiFuji.name: Azusa, Kaiji & Fuji
jrAzusaKaijiFuji.description: Scenic limited express trains offering mountain and lake views.
jrAzusaKaijiFuji.history: Iconic trains serving Japan's central tourist destinations.

jrNikkoKinugawa.name: Nikko & Kinugawa
jrNikkoKinugawa.description: Scenic trains to Nikko's temples and mountain onsen (hot springs).
jrNikkoKinugawa.history: Popular trains for visiting UNESCO World Heritage sites.

jrOdoriko.name: Odoriko
jrOdoriko.description: Scenic coastal express train with ocean and mountain views.
jrOdoriko.history: A favorite train for beach and hot spring destinations.

jrSazanamiWakashio.name: Sazanami, Wakashio & Shiosai
jrSazanamiWakashio.description: Scenic trains serving coastal Chiba with ocean views.
jrSazanamiWakashio.history: Trains connecting Tokyo to Chiba's scenic coastal areas.

jrKusatsuShima.name: Kusatsu & Shima
jrKusatsuShima.description: Mountain resort express trains offering hot spring and mountain leisure.
jrKusatsuShima.history: Premium trains for visiting Japan's famous mountain onsen.

jrInahoShirayuki.name: Inaho & Shirayuki
jrInahoShirayuki.description: Limited express trains connecting Niigata with Akita region.
jrInahoShirayuki.history: Important connections for Japan's northern regions.

saganoRomanticTrain.name: Sagano Romantic Train
saganoRomanticTrain.description: A vintage-style sightseeing train traveling through scenic bamboo groves and valleys.
saganoRomanticTrain.history: Opened in 2007 to showcase Kyoto's natural beauty.

pandaKuroshio.name: Panda Kuroshio
pandaKuroshio.description: A limited express train with panda-themed design and decorations.

slYamaguchi.name: SL Yamaguchi (Steam Locomotive)
slYamaguchi.description: A nostalgic steam train journey through rural Yamaguchi.
slYamaguchi.history: Historic steam locomotive service operating since 1974.

detectiveConanTrain.name: Detective Conan Train
detectiveConanTrain.description: A themed train decorated with Detective Conan characters for fans.

kitaroTrain.name: Kitaro Train
kitaroTrain.description: A train themed with Gegege no Kitaro characters and folklore.
kitaroTrain.history: Themed train celebrating local Yokai folklore.

westExpressGinga.name: WEST EXPRESS GINGA
westExpressGinga.description: A luxury sleeper express train with premium accommodations.
westExpressGinga.history: Modern luxury sleeper train launched in 2017.

twilightExpressMizukaze.name: Twilight Express MIZUKAZE
twilightExpressMizukaze.description: A premium overnight express train with elegant accommodations.
twilightExpressMizukaze.history: Modern successor to classic Twilight Express, launched 2021.

helloKittyHaruka.name: Hello Kitty HARUKA
helloKittyHaruka.description: A cute Hello Kitty-themed express train connecting airport to Kyoto.

berumontaTrain.name: Belles montagnes et mer
berumontaTrain.description: A scenic luxury train showcasing Toyama's mountains and coastal views.
